title: dactylorhiza sambucina f. hybrida
intro: Dactylorhiza sambucina f. hybrida, also known as the lesser marsh orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family. Traditionally, the roots of Dactylorhiza sambucina f. hybrida have been used in herbal medicine, where they are valued for their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of the plant have been used to aid in the relief of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of menopause. Dactylorhiza sambucina f. hybrida can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions for its medicinal benefits.
